meta.description: Compressed hunt cards for bridge — one-line detection rules for bulk scanning
meta.usage: Load cards into context. For each card, grep target code for card.grep. On hit: execute card.check steps directly, use validWhen/invalidWhen/impact for rapid triage, use optional preconditions/proofShape/falsePositiveSignals/reportEvidence when present, and only read full DB entry (card.ref + card.lines) for confirmed true/likely positives.

cards.title: 1. Gateway Validation Bypass
cards.detect: The `execute()` function in `AxelarExecutable` calls `_execute()` which is overridden by the child contract.
check: VERIFY: Signal 1: Critical input parameter not validated against expected range or format; Signal 2: Oracle data consumed without staleness check or sanity...
PROVE: Attacker identifies a receiver that doesn't validate via the gateway; Attacker calls execute() with forged commandId, sourceChain, sourceAddress, payload
FALSIFY: Not this bug when: Validation exists but is in an upstream function caller; Safe if: Parameter range is inherently bounded by the type or protocol invariant
ANTIPATTERN: No gateway validation — anyone can call execute
cards.antipattern: No gateway validation — anyone can call execute
cards.validWhen: Signal 1: Critical input parameter not validated against expected range or format; Signal 2: Oracle data consumed without staleness check or sanity bounds
cards.invalidWhen: Not this bug when: Validation exists but is in an upstream function caller; Safe if: Parameter range is inherently bounded by the type or protocol invariant
preconditions: attacker control of: specific conditions per pattern
Attacker identifies a receiver that doesn't validate via the gateway
Attacker calls execute() with forged commandId, sourceChain, sourceAddress, payload
Attacker drains funds or corrupts cross-chain state
cards.proofShape: Attacker identifies a receiver that doesn't validate via the gateway -> Attacker calls execute() with forged commandId, sourceChain, sourceAddress, payload -> Without validateContractCall(), the message is processed as...
falsePositiveSignals: Not this bug when: Validation exists but is in an upstream function caller
Safe if: Parameter range is inherently bounded by the type or protocol invariant
Requires attacker control of: specific conditions per pattern

cards.detect: Accepts from any source chain/address
check: VERIFY: Accepts from any source chain/address
FALSIFY: Allowlist with proper string handling
cards.antipattern: Accepts from any source chain/address
cards.validWhen: Accepts from any source chain/address

cards.detect: Revert after token burn on source = fund loss
check: VERIFY: Revert after token burn on source = fund loss
FALSIFY: Handle all failures gracefully
cards.antipattern: Revert after token burn on source = fund loss
cards.validWhen: Revert after token burn on source = fund loss

cards.title: 4. Express Execution Front-Running
cards.detect: No check if express already processed
check: VERIFY: No check if express already processed
FALSIFY: ExpressExecutable handles express vs standard coordination
cards.antipattern: No check if express already processed
cards.securePattern: ExpressExecutable handles express vs standard coordination
cards.validWhen: No check if express already processed
cards.invalidWhen: ExpressExecutable handles express vs standard coordination

cards.title: 5. ITS Token Manager Misconfiguration

FALSIFY: Mint-Burn on remote chains + flow limits

cards.title: 6. Gas Service Payment Issues
cards.detect: Message sent without gas payment — stuck forever
check: VERIFY: Message sent without gas payment — stuck forever
PROVE: Hardcoded gas doesn't account for destination chain costs
FALSIFY: Estimate gas based on destination and payload
cards.antipattern: Hardcoded gas doesn't account for destination chain costs
cards.securePattern: Estimate gas based on destination and payload
cards.validWhen: Message sent without gas payment — stuck forever
cards.invalidWhen: Estimate gas based on destination and payload
cards.proofShape: Hardcoded gas doesn't account for destination chain costs

cards.detect: Different representations of same chain
check: VERIFY: Different representations of same chain
FALSIFY: Store and compare using Axelar's exact chain IDs
cards.antipattern: Different representations of same chain

cards.validWhen: Different representations of same chain

FALSIFY: Checks-Effects-Interactions + ReentrancyGuard

cards.title: 1. Router Message Validation
cards.detect: Contracts implementing `IAny2EVMMessageReceiver` expose `ccipReceive()` as a public/external function but omit the...
check: VERIFY: Signal 1: Critical input parameter not validated against expected range or format; Signal 2: Oracle data consumed without staleness check or sanity...
PROVE: Attacker identifies a CCIP receiver without router validation; Attacker calls ccipReceive() directly with forged Client.Any2EVMMessage
FALSIFY: Not this bug when: Validation exists but is in an upstream function caller; Safe if: Parameter range is inherently bounded by the type or protocol invariant
ANTIPATTERN: Router address can be changed to attacker-controlled contract
cards.antipattern: Router address can be changed to attacker-controlled contract
cards.validWhen: Signal 1: Critical input parameter not validated against expected range or format; Signal 2: Oracle data consumed without staleness check or sanity bounds
cards.invalidWhen: Not this bug when: Validation exists but is in an upstream function caller; Safe if: Parameter range is inherently bounded by the type or protocol invariant
preconditions: attacker control of: specific conditions per pattern
Attacker identifies a CCIP receiver without router validation
Attacker calls ccipReceive() directly with forged Client.Any2EVMMessage
Forged message contains attacker-controlled sender, sourceChainSelector, and data
Attacker drains funds or corrupts state
cards.proofShape: Attacker identifies a CCIP receiver without router validation -> Attacker calls ccipReceive() directly with forged Client.Any2EVMMessage -> Forged message contains attacker-controlled sender, sourceChainSelector, and...
falsePositiveSignals: Not this bug when: Validation exists but is in an upstream function caller
Safe if: Parameter range is inherently bounded by the type or protocol invariant
Requires attacker control of: specific conditions per pattern

cards.title: 4. Extra Args & Gas Limit Misconfiguration
cards.detect: Empty extraArgs defaults to 200k gas
check: VERIFY: Empty extraArgs defaults to 200k gas
PROVE: Using V1 when V2 is needed (missing strict sequencing control)
FALSIFY: Right gas limit with V2 extra args

cards.validWhen: Empty extraArgs defaults to 200k gas
cards.invalidWhen: Right gas limit with V2 extra args

FALSIFY: Never revert, store failed messages for recovery

preconditions: ccipReceive reverts on the destination chain, the message enters a failed state in the OffRamp.

cards.title: 1. Cross-Chain Replay Attacks

FALSIFY: Not this bug when: Validation exists but is in an upstream function caller; Safe if: Parameter range is inherently bounded by the type or protocol invariant
IMPACT: Double Minting: Tokens minted multiple times for single deposit
cards.antipattern: No contract address in replay protection
cards.validWhen: Signal 1: Critical input parameter not validated against expected range or format; Signal 2: Oracle data consumed without staleness check or sanity bounds
cards.invalidWhen: Not this bug when: Validation exists but is in an upstream function caller; Safe if: Parameter range is inherently bounded by the type or protocol invariant

Attacker replays same signature on Chain B
Attacker replays message on Instance B
cards.proofShape: *Scenario 1: Cross-Chain Signature Replay -> User signs message to bridge tokens on Chain A -> Relayer executes on Chain A successfully

cards.title: 1. ISM Validation Vulnerabilities
cards.detect: Contracts that receive Hyperlane messages fail to configure or validate the ISM properly, allowing arbitrary message...
check: VERIFY: Signal 1: Critical input parameter not validated against expected range or format; Signal 2: Oracle data consumed without staleness check or sanity...
PROVE: Protocol deploys Hyperlane receiver without setting ISM; Default ISM may be permissive or not configured
FALSIFY: Not this bug when: Validation exists but is in an upstream function caller; Safe if: Parameter range is inherently bounded by the type or protocol invariant
IMPACT: Message Forgery: Arbitrary messages processed as legitimate
cards.antipattern: Always returns true
cards.securePattern: Deploy with MultisigISM for strong security
cards.validWhen: Signal 1: Critical input parameter not validated against expected range or format; Signal 2: Oracle data consumed without staleness check or sanity bounds
cards.invalidWhen: Not this bug when: Validation exists but is in an upstream function caller; Safe if: Parameter range is inherently bounded by the type or protocol invariant
cards.impact: Message Forgery: Arbitrary messages processed as legitimate
preconditions: attacker control of: specific conditions per pattern
Attacker calls Mailbox.process() with forged message
Attacker mints tokens or corrupts state
cards.proofShape: Protocol deploys Hyperlane receiver without setting ISM -> Default ISM may be permissive or not configured -> Attacker calls Mailbox.process() with forged message
falsePositiveSignals: Not this bug when: Validation exists but is in an upstream function caller
Safe if: Parameter range is inherently bounded by the type or protocol invariant
Requires attacker control of: specific conditions per pattern

cards.title: 2. Message Replay Attacks
cards.detect: When message processing fails (reverts), the protocol may not mark the message as consumed.
check: VERIFY: When message processing fails (reverts), the protocol may not mark the message as consumed.
PROVE: Attacker initiates cross-chain token transfer; On destination, message processing partially succeeds but then reverts
FALSIFY: Mark processed before state changes
ANTIPATTERN: State changed before potential revert
cards.antipattern: State changed before potential revert
cards.validWhen: When message processing fails (reverts), the protocol may not mark the message as consumed.
preconditions: message processing fails (reverts), the protocol may not mark the message as consumed.
Attacker initiates cross-chain token transfer
Attacker retries message processing
cards.proofShape: Attacker initiates cross-chain token transfer -> On destination, message processing partially succeeds but then reverts -> Message ID not marked as processed due to revert

cards.title: 1. Channel Blocking Vulnerabilities
cards.detect: Protocols inherit from `LzApp` without implementing the non-blocking pattern (`NonblockingLzApp`), or they implement it...
check: VERIFY: Signal 1: Critical input parameter not validated against expected range or format; Signal 2: Oracle data consumed without staleness check or sanity...
PROVE: Attacker identifies a cross-chain contract without non-blocking implementation; Attacker sends a message designed to revert on destination (insufficient gas, malicious...
FALSIFY: Not this bug when: Validation exists but is in an upstream function caller; Safe if: Parameter range is inherently bounded by the type or protocol invariant
IMPACT: Channel DoS: All messages from affected source chain are blocked
cards.antipattern: Inherits LzApp directly without non-blocking wrapper
cards.validWhen: Signal 1: Critical input parameter not validated against expected range or format; Signal 2: Oracle data consumed without staleness check or sanity bounds
cards.invalidWhen: Not this bug when: Validation exists but is in an upstream function caller; Safe if: Parameter range is inherently bounded by the type or protocol invariant
cards.impact: Channel DoS: All messages from affected source chain are blocked

Attacker identifies a cross-chain contract without non-blocking implementation
Attacker sends a message designed to revert on destination (insufficient gas, malicious payload)
cards.proofShape: Attacker identifies a cross-chain contract without non-blocking implementation -> Attacker sends a message designed to revert on destination (insufficient gas, malicious payload) -> Message fails and is stored in...

Safe if: Parameter range is inherently bounded by the type or protocol invariant

cards.title: 2. Minimum Gas Validation Vulnerabilities
cards.detect: Protocols allow users to specify arbitrary `adapterParams` without validating the gas limit meets minimum requirements...
check: VERIFY: Protocols allow users to specify arbitrary adapterParams without validating the gas limit meets minimum requirements for the destination chain...
PROVE: Attacker calls a cross-chain function with minimal gas in adapterParams; LayerZero relayer delivers message with specified gas
FALSIFY: Use built-in minimum gas configuration
ANTIPATTERN: User controls gas amount without validation
cards.antipattern: User controls gas amount without validation
cards.validWhen: Protocols allow users to specify arbitrary adapterParams without validating the gas limit meets minimum requirements for the destination chain operation.
preconditions: Attacker calls a cross-chain function with minimal gas in adapterParams
cards.proofShape: Attacker calls a cross-chain function with minimal gas in adapterParams -> LayerZero relayer delivers message with specified gas -> Transaction reverts due to out-of-gas before reaching NonblockingLzApp catch

cards.title: 2. Guardian Set Vulnerabilities
cards.detect: Protocols don't account for guardian set transitions properly.
